claims.claim_text: Ollama provides simple local installation and exposes a local REST API for running and managing models.
claims.span_hint: section: Four Useful Runtime Roles > Ollama
claims.rationale: The Ollama README directly documents local installation paths and its REST API.

claims.claim_text: MLX and MLX LM are designed for Apple Silicon and provide a native local path for running LLM workloads on Macs.
claims.span_hint: section: Four Useful Runtime Roles > MLX
claims.rationale: The MLX LM project explicitly presents itself as a package for generating text and fine-tuning LLMs with MLX on Apple silicon.

claims.claim_text: Hugging Face Transformers supports direct model loading and pipeline-based inference, making it suitable for flexible experimentation and engineering workflows.
claims.span_hint: section: Four Useful Runtime Roles > Hugging Face Transformers
claims.rationale: The Transformers quickstart directly covers loading pretrained models and using pipelines for inference.

claims.claim_text: vLLM is an inference and serving engine oriented toward high-throughput LLM serving patterns.
claims.span_hint: section: Four Useful Runtime Roles > vLLM
claims.rationale: The vLLM README directly describes it as a high-throughput inference and serving engine for LLMs.

further_reading.title: Ollama README
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful for understanding Ollama's install flow, CLI, and local API model.

further_reading.title: MLX LM README
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows the Apple Silicon-focused MLX path for local LLM inference and related workflows.

further_reading.title: Transformers Quickstart
further_reading.why_relevant: Covers the core abstractions that make Transformers useful for direct engineering experimentation.

further_reading.title: vLLM README
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains the serving-focused design and throughput-oriented features behind vLLM.

notes: Most of the module is pedagogical guidance and repeated scenario framing. The main inline-citation needs are the four runtime capability mappings above; later stage/default examples mostly restate those same supported capabilities.
